name: Guardo a la Presa de Compuerto a Santuario de Nuestra Señora del Brezo — circular desde Muñeca
region_intro: Mountain biking around Guardo offers diverse terrain within the Montaña Palentina region of Palencia, Spain. The area is situated on the banks of the Carrión river and the southern slopes of the Sierra del Brezo, providing varied topography. Elevations range from approximately 1,100 meters near the river to 1,836 meters at Peña Mayor de Guardo. This landscape features winding trails, forested areas, and old mining paths, making it suitable for a range of mountain biking experiences. Best mountain bike trails around Guardo The most popular mountain bike trail is Campos de la Peña a Velilla del Río Carrión a Roblón del Monasterio — circular desde Guardo, a difficult 35.4 miles (56.9 km) trail that takes 5 hours 55 minutes to complete, traversing varied landscapes including the Roblón del Monasterio. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Las Doncellas del Hoyal Trail Loop, a moderate 16.8 miles (27.0 km) path. This route features a significant elevation gain and leads through scenic natural areas. Local mountain bikers also love the Guardo a la Presa de Compuerto a Santuario de Nuestra Señora del Brezo — circular desde Muñeca, a 25.2 miles (40.5 km) trail leading through the Montaña Palentina, often completed in about 3 hours 28 minutes. Mountain biking around Guardo is defined by river banks, mountain slopes, and forested areas, with trails that include both natural paths and old mining routes. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy riverside rides to challenging mountain ascents. The routes in Guardo are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from 1 review. More than 290 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Guardo's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Guardo?
A: There are over 35 mountain bike routes around Guardo, catering to various skill levels. You'll find a good mix, including 3 easy routes, 25 moderate routes, and 11 difficult trails for experienced riders.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for beginners or families in Guardo?
A: Yes, Guardo offers several easy mountain bike trails perfect for beginners or families. One such route is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1326593470/guardo-a-villalba-de-guardo-circular-por-el-rio-carrion" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guardo a Villalba de Guardo — circular por el Río Carrión&lt;/a&gt;, which follows the Carrión river and provides a gentle ride. The region is also developing new trails specifically designed to be accessible for families and beginners.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Guardo?
A: The terrain around Guardo is quite diverse, ranging from river banks along the Carrión to the southern slopes of the Sierra del Brezo. You'll encounter winding trails, forested areas, and even old mining paths. Elevations vary from approximately 1,100 meters near the river to 1,836 meters at Peña Mayor de Guardo, offering varied topography for your rides.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in Guardo?
A: Yes, many of the mountain bike routes around Guardo are circular. For example, the moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1327276615/guardo-a-la-presa-de-compuerto-a-santuario-de-nuestra-senora-del-brezo-circular-desde-muneca" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guardo a la Presa de Compuerto a Santuario de Nuestra Señora del Brezo — circular desde Muñeca&lt;/a&gt; offers a scenic loop through Montaña Palentina. Another option is the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1170441631/mantinos-roblon-del-monasterio-velilla-del-rio-carrion-circular-por-parque-natural-montana-palentina" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mantinos, Roblón del Monasterio, Velilla del Río Carrión — circular por Parque Natural Montaña Palentina&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What natural attractions or landmarks can I see while mountain biking near Guardo?
A: The region offers several natural attractions and landmarks. You can visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5200388" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Roblón of El Monasterio&lt;/a&gt;, a notable natural monument. The area is also home to the Mazobre Waterfall, the largest waterfall in Montaña Palentina, accessible via an easy trail. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5649404" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sanctuary of Our Lady of Brezo&lt;/a&gt; is another significant point of interest.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about the trails in Guardo?
A: The mountain bike trails in Guardo are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars. Riders often praise the diverse landscapes, which include winding trails, forested areas, and old mining paths, providing a varied and engaging experience for all skill levels.

Q: Are there any challenging mountain bike trails for advanced riders in Guardo?
A: Yes, Guardo has several challenging routes for advanced mountain bikers. The difficult &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1326497424/campos-de-la-pena-a-velilla-del-rio-carrion-a-roblon-del-monasterio-circular-desde-guardo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Campos de la Peña a Velilla del Río Carrión a Roblón del Monasterio — circular desde Guardo&lt;/a&gt;, for instance, covers over 56 km with significant elevation gain. Other routes near Mantinos and Velilla del Río Carrión also offer demanding rides requiring good fitness and technical skills.

Q: Is there parking available at the trailheads in Guardo?
A: While specific trailhead parking details vary by route, Guardo is a municipality with infrastructure to support outdoor activities. It's advisable to check individual route descriptions on komoot for precise parking information, but generally, you can find parking options within or near the town before heading out to the trails.

Q: What is the best time of year to go mountain biking in Guardo?
A: The Montaña Palentina region, where Guardo is located, offers a rich landscape for outdoor activities. Spring and autumn generally provide the most pleasant weather for mountain biking, with milder temperatures and vibrant scenery. Summer can also be good, but it's wise to start earlier in the day to avoid the midday heat. Winter riding is possible, but conditions can be more challenging due to colder temperatures and potential snow at higher elevations.

Q: Are there any trails that lead to scenic viewpoints in Guardo?
A: Many trails in the Guardo area offer scenic viewpoints due to the varied topography, with elevations reaching up to 1,836 meters at Peña Mayor de Guardo. Routes that traverse the Sierra del Brezo or lead to higher points in the Montaña Palentina will often provide expansive views of the surrounding natural beauty and landscapes.

Q: How long do mountain bike trails typically take to complete in Guardo?
A: The duration of mountain bike trails in Guardo varies significantly based on difficulty and distance. Easy routes, like the one along the Carrión river, might take around 1.5 to 2 hours. Moderate trails, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1326528462/las-doncellas-del-hoyal-trail-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Las Doncellas del Hoyal Trail Loop&lt;/a&gt;, typically take around 2.5 to 3 hours. More difficult and longer routes can take 5 to 6 hours or even more to complete.
tips: Muñeca is a town just 3 kilometers from Guardo. It has a small population of less than 100 inhabitants. It's not a bad place to start a walk or leave the car when you go out on an adventure. It is easily accessible from the Puerto de Cerredo - Aguilar de Campo Highway.
Guardo is a great place to start an adventure because it is the gateway to the Montaña Palentina Natural Park. It sits on the banks of the Carrión river.
At one time, this was a mining town with a larger population. Today it is a great place to start a mountain adventure or simply enjoy the beauty of the area.

Built in 1960, next to the Camporredondo reservoir, it guarantees the irrigation of 50,000 hectares of crops. It forms part of the "Ruta de los Pantanos" next to the Aguilar de Campoo, Ruesga, Requejada and Camporredondo reservoirs.
It can be climbed from Valcobero, from Valsurbio and from the Sanctuary of the Virgen del Brezo

The Sanctuary of the Virgen del Brezo, located on the Palencia Mountain, is a temple of great devotion and the scene of a popular pilgrimage. At an altitude of 1,400 m, in the Sierra del Brezo, the sanctuary houses the Romanesque carving of the Virgin of Brezo. Built in the 13th century and renovated in the 18th century, the sanctuary has a church, chapel and guest house, and offers peace and natural beauty to its visitors.

waypoints.name: Sanctuary of Our Lady of Brezo
